hooks.note: Top-nav Betting dropdown. The <ul> id (e.g. navItemyVsThiAx, navItemkIp_cbPR, navItemmvA-OYI7) is randomized per request -> match by structure/href, not id. The toggle anchor points to /betting/analytics. Confirmed.
The Betting dropdown toggle link itself (href=/betting/analytics). Has data-nav-sub-menu-toggle-target to a randomized id.

Betting dropdown items: /en-us/betting, /en-gb/betting, /en-in/betting, /pt-br/apostas, /sv-se/betting, /da-dk/betting, /betting, /betting/money (Compare odds), /betting/analytics (Analytics). Match by href substring betting|apostas.
Betting forum link inside the Forums dropdown menu. Separate from the Betting dropdown.
Confirmed. Container div with class betting-section and id=betting on individual match pages. Holds fantasy/betting promo content; inner tracked slot data-lnhnyipxec="FantasyGamesList".
Betting-adjacent block immediately after div.betting-section. Contains the Analytics center CTA and the Pick-a-winner vote. Has data-content-block attribute.
Analytics center card inside analytics-pick-a-winner. Headline 'Analytics center', subtext 'Find insights for your bet', links to /betting/analytics/{id}/...
The clickable 'Go to analytics center' link to the betting analytics/odds page for this match.
Community 'Pick a winner' prediction/vote widget (win-probability bars). Betting-adjacent; nested inside analytics-pick-a-winner. Hide separately if vote feature should remain.

Durable wrapper class for the ad/banner slot placed right after the betting section (data-trdswuppiz="BannerOnIndividualMatchPageDesktopMobileSalesNEW"). Wrapper class is stable; the inner ad div class (BZ4Bl4KkTN) and /out2/ link are randomized ads.

hooks.where: matches page (also used in match listings/results widgets)
hooks.note: Per-row 'Odds & prediction' button (bar-chart icon) on every match row in the /matches listing. title="Odds & prediction for match", href=/betting/analytics/{id}/... One per fixture. Most numerous listing hook.

Hidden impression-tracked slot for a featured bookmaker/provider in the matches header. data-trdswuppiz attribute name is randomized/hashed; the VALUE 'MATCHES_V3_FEATURED_PROVIDER' is the stable signal -> match by [data-imp-trk] with value containing PROVIDER. Lives inside div.match-fixtures-source (hidden).
Top-level wrapper around the whole bookmaker comparison/ranking list on the betting hub page.
Container holding all bookmaker bonus rows (data-loop-items=24). 'bc' = Better Collective. Inside div.bcb-atts.

One per bookmaker: bonus-code-block--ggbet-e-sports, --1xbet-e-sports, --bet365-e-sports, --stake-com-e-sports, --bc-game-e-sports, --betsio-com-e-sports, --zizobet-e-sports, --granawin-e-sports, etc. Class prefix 'bonus-code-block--' is durable; the bookmaker suffix varies.

hooks.selector: span.c-operator-name, img.c-operator-logo, span.c-bonus-headline, ul.c-usps, span.c-bonus-tc, div.compliance-wrapper
hooks.note: Inner parts of each bonus block: operator name/logo, bonus headline (e.g. '100% up to 200 EUR'), USP bullets, T&C compliance text. All durable 'c-' prefixed classes from the Better Collective bonus component.

Outbound affiliate 'Claim Offer'/'Visit site' links to bookmakers via the bcwp.hltv.org redirector (e.g. /goto/bet365/E-sports, /goto/ggbet/E-sports). rel=nofollow. bcwp = Better Collective WordPress betting subdomain.

All in-body betting links: /betting/analytics, /betting/sites, /betting/sites/esports, /betting/money, /betting/online-casinos (+/plinko-game, /aviator-game), /betting/responsible-gambling/* . Broad fallback selector by href substring betting|apostas.
Bookmaker logo/creative images served from assets-bcwp.hltv.org (Better Collective asset host), e.g. operator square logos in bonus blocks.

Third-party 'ADM Smart Pixel - Better Collective' betting-affiliate tracking script (cadmus.script.ac). Path id (d23sa75evsxrsv) is account-specific. Block by domain. kind=iframe used loosely for external script.

Header-bidding ad loader (htlbid.com/v3/hltv.org/htlbid.js) that injects the betting banner ads (/out2/ creatives). Not exclusively betting, but it is the delivery vehicle for the bookmaker banner ads. Block at extension's discretion.

Impression-tracked banner ad creatives (often bookmaker ads) inside randomized-class wrappers (e.g. BZ4Bl4KkTN) with randomized data-* attrs (data-gggsdghytj, data-trdswuppiz, data-hixuoqkfqi). These are ad placements not native betting markup; included because they frequently carry betting banners. Match by /out2/ href + data-imp-trk.

keywords.note: Core term. Can appear in editorial CS news (e.g. 'betting scandal', 'match-fixing/betting fraud') but strongly signals gambling sections, sponsor links and CTAs. Med risk as free text; treat hits in href/alt as high-confidence.
CTA phrasing almost exclusively used by sportsbooks. Rare in editorial prose. Strong signal in button/anchor text.
CTA / instructional gambling phrase. Very rarely in CS news prose. High-confidence signal.
Direct gambling call-to-action. Near-zero false positives on a stats/news site.
Common in betting widgets but also appears in prose ('odds of winning', 'against all odds', 'tournament odds'). Med risk as free text; pair with brand/'betting' context to confirm.
Almost always gambling-specific. Low risk anywhere including prose. Strong reliable signal.
Plural of bookmaker. Equally low risk, gambling-specific.
Gambling-specific but occasionally figurative ('I'd wager that...'). Mostly safe; med risk in editorial prose, low in href/UI.
Sportsbook UI element. One word; never appears in CS editorial. Low risk anywhere — high-confidence signal.
Two-word variant of betslip. Gambling-specific, low risk anywhere.
Promotional gambling offer. Rare in non-betting context. Low risk.
Plural promo term. Gambling-specific, low risk.
Casino/sportsbook promo phrase. Strong gambling signal, low false positives.
Gambling/casino onboarding promo. Low risk; rare elsewhere on a CS site.
No-hyphen variant of sign-up bonus. Gambling promo, low risk.
Standard casino/sportsbook onboarding offer. Low risk, strong signal.
Betting feature (cash out an open bet). Also appears in skin-trading/payment prose. Med risk; href/UI scope safest.
Two-word betting feature term. Can appear in general finance/withdrawal prose. Med risk; prefer attr/href.
Multi-leg bet (UK term). Gambling-specific; essentially never in CS news. Low risk anywhere.
Multi-leg bet (US term). Gambling-specific, low risk anywhere.
US betting market type. Gambling-specific, low false positives.
Betting market (Asian/European handicap) but also a common everyday word ('mechanical handicap', accessibility, map handicap). High risk as free text — restrict to href/attr to avoid prose hits.
Totals betting market. Also used generically ('over/under 16 rounds'). Med risk; safer with betting context nearby.

HLTV has legitimate community match-prediction features unrelated to money, plus editorial 'predictions'. High risk as text; only treat as betting signal in href to a bookmaker or 'betting-predictions' path.
Gambling content phrase. Low risk; strongly indicates a betting/affiliate page.
Compound disambiguates 'odds'. Gambling-specific, low risk.
Affiliate/listicle phrasing for bookmaker directories. Low risk, strong signal.
Affiliate CTA phrasing. Gambling-specific, low risk.
Comparison/affiliate phrase. Disambiguated compound of 'odds'; low risk.
Portuguese for 'bets/betting'. Strong PT-market signal (Betano/Betsson PT pages). Med risk only because it can appear in Brazilian editorial about betting controversies; mostly reliable.
Portuguese for 'bookmaker/betting house'. Gambling-specific, low risk.
German for 'bets/to bet'. Strong DE-market signal. Med risk as it can be a verb in prose; reliable in href/UI.
German 'sports betting'. Compound, gambling-specific, low risk.
Direct term but appears in editorial (responsible-gambling notices, 'gambling addiction', regulatory news). Med risk as text; high-confidence in href/affiliate paths.
Gambling term but also appears in brand/event names and map/venue prose ('Casino map', sponsor names like 'N1 Casino', 'Stake Casino'). High risk as free text — prefer href/attr scope.
Affiliate/casino bonus phrasing, but also legit e-commerce/merch discounts. Med risk; combine with brand for confidence.
Casino/sportsbook offer phrase; can also be generic merch/promo. Med risk; brand-adjacency raises confidence.

Both the Stake.com brand AND a betting amount AND ordinary prose ('high stakes', 'Stake Ranked' events, 'at stake'). High risk as free text — match only in href to stake.com/affiliate domains or attr.
Betting/casino term but also prize-pool, skin-trading and salary prose ('tournament payout', 'prize payout'). High risk as free text — restrict to href/attr near a bookmaker.

Substring of many non-gambling words ('Tibet', 'alphabet', 'betray') and ordinary verb usage. High risk as free text — only treat as signal in href/attr or as a whole-word CTA, ideally anchored to a bookmaker domain.
Casino promo term but extremely common generically ('bonus round', 'bonus content', 'XP bonus' in games). High risk as free text — prefer href/attr scope, brand-adjacent.
Casino/skin-gambling term, but also figurative ('hit the jackpot') and game-mode names. Med risk; href scope safest.
Casino game and skin-gambling mode (CSGO roulette sites). Med risk; can appear in unrelated prose but strongly gambling-leaning on a CS site.
Casino bonus T&C phrase. Gambling-specific, low risk anywhere.
Sportsbook promotion. Disambiguated compound; low risk.
In-play sportsbook product. Gambling-specific, low risk.
Live-betting term but can describe gameplay generically. Med risk; href/betting context safest.
Mandatory footer/disclaimer on betting pages. Low risk and a near-certain indicator that gambling content/sponsorship is present on the page.
Age-gate badge accompanying gambling ads/sponsors. Med risk (also appears for mature content generally); strong when near a bookmaker brand or 'begambleaware'.
UK responsible-gambling org linked in betting ad footers (begambleaware.org). Low risk; certain gambling indicator.
Standard betting-ad disclaimer. Low risk, certain gambling indicator.
Often accompanies bonus/promo offers but also generic. Med risk; strong when paired with 'bonus'/'free bet'.
Casino/sportsbook CTA. Gambling-specific, low risk.
Sportsbook same-game-parlay product. Gambling-specific, low risk.
Betting market (outright winner). Also generic adjective/adverb. Med risk; betting context or href safest.
Casino onboarding bonus phrase. Med risk; strong when paired with 'bonus'/'match'.
Deposit-match casino promo, but 'match' collides heavily with CS match context. Med risk — only a signal alongside 'deposit'/'bonus'/brand.
CS-specific gambling category (case/skin betting). Low risk, strong signal.
CS skin-gambling mechanic offered by case sites (Hellcase/Key-Drop). Also legitimate in-game feature. Med risk; href to a case-site domain confirms.
Skin-gambling game mode. Also figurative ('it's a coinflip'). Med risk; gambling-leaning on a CS site, href safest.
Casino 'crash' product common on CS gambling sites. Low risk, gambling-specific.
Gambling funding action but also generic finance/account term. High risk as free text — restrict to href/attr near a bookmaker/casino.
Casino cashier term but also generic ('team withdrawal from event', account withdrawal). High risk as free text — prefer href/attr scope.
Explicit category phrase. Gambling-specific, low risk, very strong signal.
Game-specific betting phrase used by affiliates/bookmakers. Low risk, strong signal.
Legacy game-specific betting phrase. Low risk, strong signal.
Betting-tips jargon. Gambling-specific, low risk.
Betting-arbitrage technique phrase. Gambling-specific, low risk.
Point-spread betting market but extremely common generic word ('spread of the meta', 'spread out'). High risk as free text — only a signal in href/attr with betting context.
Totals betting market but also a plain word ('round totals', stats totals on a stats site). High risk as free text — restrict to href/betting-widget scope.

overblockRisks.why: Substring matching would catch 'Tibet', 'alphabet', 'betray', 'better', 'BetBoom' inside non-betting URLs, plus the verb 'bet' in editorial. Even scoped to href, a naive substring test hits /results?event=...alphabet... style slugs and team/event names. As free text it is catastrophic.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Match only as a whole word (\bbet\b) AND only inside href/src/attr, AND require the href host to be a known bookmaker/affiliate domain (bcwp.hltv.org, stake.com, etc.) or the path to start with /betting. Never match 'bet' as a bare substring anywhere.

overblockRisks.why: HLTV runs events literally named 'Stake Ranked' and prose like 'at stake', 'high stakes', 'stakes were high'. Brand-matching 'Stake' in an event/tournament name would hide entire legitimate match rows and event pages.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Only treat 'stake' as a signal when href points to stake.com or a bcwp affiliate redirect. Brand 'Stake' in visible text (event names, prose) must NEVER hide a match/row/article — at most flag an attached odds/affiliate widget.

overblockRisks.why: 'odds of winning', 'against all odds', 'the odds favor', 'tournament odds' appear in editorial and analysis prose. Hiding text nodes containing 'odds' would gut articles.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Do not hide free text on 'odds' alone. Only act when 'odds' is inside a betting widget container (div.betting-section, matchpage-analytics-section) or paired with a bookmaker brand/href in the same block. Prefer the compound forms 'betting odds'/'best odds'/'odds boost'.

overblockRisks.why: HLTV has a legitimate community 'Pick a winner' prediction feature and editorial 'predictions' articles unrelated to money. Match-prediction is a core non-betting feature.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Only treat as betting when href contains /betting/ or a bookmaker domain. The pick-a-winner-wrapper must be hidden ONLY by its own dedicated selector if the user opts to remove the vote, never via the 'predictions' keyword on text.

overblockRisks.why: Appears in brand/event/sponsor names ('N1 Casino', 'Stake Casino') and could be a map/venue reference in prose. As free text it hides sponsor mentions inside legit articles.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Restrict strictly to href/src containing /casino or a casino domain. Never match 'casino' in visible text or in event-name attributes that gate a whole match row.

overblockRisks.why: Common everyday/accessibility word and a generic gameplay term ('map handicap'), not just the Asian-handicap betting market.
overblockRisks.mitigation: href/attr only, and only within a betting widget or bookmaker-domain link. Never match in prose.

overblockRisks.why: All are extremely common generic words on a stats/news site: 'spread of the meta', 'round totals', stats 'totals' columns, 'tournament payout/prize payout', account 'deposit', 'team withdrawal from event', 'XP bonus'/'bonus round'/'bonus content'. Any text-scope matching destroys stats tables and editorial.
overblockRisks.mitigation: href/attr only AND require bookmaker/casino domain or /betting path adjacency. Explicitly exclude stats-table cells and 'withdrawal' near event/roster context. Never hide a stats column header or row on these terms.

overblockRisks.why: Core betting term but appears in legitimate journalism: 'betting scandal', 'match-fixing/betting fraud ban', 'gambling sponsorship controversy'. Hiding any element containing 'betting' would remove news articles and forum threads that are legit content to keep.
overblockRisks.mitigation: In free text, do not hide article/forum body or headlines on 'betting' alone. Treat as high-confidence only in href/alt/title or inside known betting containers. News/forum article bodies are a protected zone.

overblockRisks.why: Portuguese/German for betting; can appear in localized editorial about betting controversies, not only on bookmaker pages.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Same as 'betting' — protect article/forum bodies; act confidently only in href (/apostas path, bookmaker domain) or dedicated betting containers.

overblockRisks.why: Age-gate also used for mature/violent content generally, not exclusively gambling.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Only a signal when adjacent to a bookmaker brand or 'begambleaware'/'gamble responsibly' in the same block. Never hide on '18+' alone.

overblockRisks.why: Describes live gameplay generically ('in-play stats', 'in-play clutch') as well as live betting.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Require betting context (betting container or bookmaker href) in the same block; never hide prose on 'in-play' alone.

overblockRisks.why: Substring '/betting' also matches '/forums/betting' (a community forum, arguably legit discussion) and could match any future path containing the substring. Hiding the link element is fine, but if a script climbs to a parent row/article it could hide legit listings.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Hide only the anchor element itself (and its immediate icon/label), never an ancestor article/match row. Treat /forums/betting as a forum link the user may want to keep — make it a separate toggle.

overblockRisks.signal: DOM hook div[data-imp-trk] a[href^="/out2/"] and htlbid.com/out2 creatives
overblockRisks.why: /out2/ is a generic outbound-ad redirector carrying ANY banner ad, not only bookmaker ads. Hiding all of them overblocks non-betting advertisers (hardware, events, merch).
overblockRisks.mitigation: Only hide /out2/ creatives whose alt/title/nearby text or destination matches a bookmaker brand or betting keyword. Otherwise leave to a general ad-blocker, not the betting filter.

overblockRisks.why: This 'Odds & prediction' button sits inside an otherwise-legitimate match row. A script that hides the row instead of the button removes a real fixture.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Hide ONLY the button/column, never the parent match row. The fixture, teams, time, and result must remain visible.

overblockRisks.signal: Brand list applied to visible text (Stake, Rivalry, Betway, Pinnacle, Buff, 1win, Pari, Leon, etc.)
overblockRisks.why: Many brands are ordinary words or event/team sponsors: 'Rivalry' (also a generic noun and an event organizer), 'Pinnacle' (generic), 'Buff' (game-balance term + Buff163 skin marketplace), '1win'/'Pari'/'Leon'/'Pixel.bet'/'Spin.bet' collide with names/words. Event names like 'Thunderpick World Championship', 'Betboom Dacha', 'Rivalry Cup' are LEGIT tournaments.
overblockRisks.mitigation: Brand-in-event-name or brand-in-prose must NOT hide the match, article, or row — only an attached odds/affiliate widget or a link whose href is the bookmaker domain. Restrict bare brand text-matching to known betting containers; prefer matching brands in href/src.

recommendations: Establish protected zones the content script must NEVER hide via keyword/brand matching: <article> bodies, news/editorial text, forum post bodies and thread titles, stats tables (and their column headers/cells), and match-listing row metadata (team names, scores, times, event names). Only dedicated betting-container selectors and bookmaker-domain href matches may act inside these zones, and even then only on the betting sub-element.

Two-tier confidence: (a) STRUCTURAL hooks (div.betting-section, #betting, div.analytics-pick-a-winner, matchpage-analytics-section, a.matchpage-analytics-center-container, category-operator-loop-wrapper, bonus-code-block--*, bcwp.hltv.org links, assets-bcwp.hltv.org images) are high-confidence — hide directly. (b) KEYWORD/BRAND hits are low-confidence — only act when inside a structural betting container OR when the keyword is in an href/src pointing to a bookmaker/affiliate domain or /betting|/apostas path.

Match-listing rows: hide ONLY the per-row 'Odds & prediction' button (a.match-btn.match-analytics-btn) — never climb to or hide the enclosing match row/fixture. The match, teams, time, and result stay visible.

Never hide an ancestor of a betting link. For a[href*="/betting"]/a[href*="/apostas"] and /out2/ creatives, hide the anchor (and its inline icon/label) only; do not walk up to a row, card, or article container.

Brand handling: a bookmaker brand appearing in an event/tournament name (e.g. 'Stake Ranked', 'Thunderpick World Championship', 'Betboom Dacha', 'Rivalry Cup') must NOT hide the match, row, results entry, or article. Only hide an odds/affiliate widget or a link whose href resolves to the bookmaker's domain. Maintain an event-name allowlist context: if the brand string is inside a link to /events/ or /matches/ it is a tournament reference, keep it.

Treat all high-risk keywords (bet, stake, bonus, deposit, withdrawal, payout, casino, handicap, spread, totals, predictions) as href/src/attr-only AND require either a bookmaker/affiliate host (bcwp.hltv.org, assets-bcwp.hltv.org, stake.com, ggbet, 1xbet, bet365, etc.) or a /betting|/apostas path segment. Combine 'bet' with whole-word boundaries (\bbet\b) even in attributes.
'odds' alone is never sufficient: require it to co-occur with a bookmaker brand/href or sit inside a betting container. Prefer the disambiguated compounds 'betting odds', 'best odds', 'odds boost'.

Protect journalism: any element identified as a news article, blog, or forum thread (URL paths /news/, /blog/, /forums/) is exempt from keyword/brand hiding entirely; betting discussion as content is legitimate. Only inline injected affiliate widgets/ads within those pages may be removed via structural hooks.
Keep /forums/betting (the betting forum link) as a SEPARATE, optional toggle distinct from the gambling-promo blocking, since it is community discussion rather than a sportsbook CTA.

For impression/ad slots that are randomized (data-imp-trk, /out2/ creatives, MATCHES_V3_FEATURED_PROVIDER, htlbid.com): only remove when the creative's alt/title/destination matches a bookmaker brand or betting keyword. Do not blanket-hide all ads under the betting filter — that overreaches into non-betting advertisers.
Localized terms (apostas, wetten, casa de apostas, sportwetten): apply the same href/container scoping. 'apostas'/'wetten' as bare verbs in localized editorial must not hide article text.

Pick-a-winner / community vote (div.pick-a-winner-wrapper) is betting-adjacent but a non-money community feature — hide it only behind its own opt-in toggle, never as a side effect of betting-section removal, so users who want the free vote keep it.
